display.purpose: Screen titles, primary CTA buttons. Uppercase-only. Never used for body text or supporting labels.

body.purpose: All UI text, answer labels, body copy, secondary buttons. The working voice of the system.

label.purpose: Scores, counters, question indexes, status glyphs, metadata. Data-voice only — never for paragraph text.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for color and opacity state transitions.

motion.name: ease-out-quart
motion.purpose: Entry animations (fadeSlideUp, fadeIn). Fast settle, no bounce.

motion.name: progress-fill
motion.purpose: Progress bar width transition on question advance.

breakpoints.name: content-max
breakpoints.purpose: max-width of the quiz content column on wider screens.

components.name: Primary Action Button
components.description: Full-width CTA for the primary action on each screen (Begin Training, Next Target, Redeploy). Amber on near-black, Bebas Neue, all-caps.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 100%; min-height: 56px; padding: 0 24px; background: #f59e0b; color: #070b14; font-family: 'Bebas Neue', sans-serif; font-size: 1.4rem; letter-spacing: 0.14em; border: none; border-radius: 2px; cursor: pointer; transition: filter 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), transform 0.1s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { filter: brightness(1.08); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.98); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f59e0b; outline-offset: 3px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { background: rgba(30,41,59,0.7); color: #334155; cursor: not-allowed; transform: none; filter: none; }

components.name: Answer Option — Default
components.description: Quiz answer button before the player has selected. Dark glass panel, amber letter badge, vehicle name + country stacked.
components.css: .ds-answer { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 14px; width: 100%; min-height: 52px; padding: 11px 14px; border-radius: 2px; border: 1px solid; cursor: pointer; font-family: 'Rajdhani', sans-serif; text-align: left; transition: border-color 0.15s, background 0.15s; } .ds-answer--default { background: rgba(15,23,42,0.85); border-color: rgba(245,158,11,0.2); color: #e2e8f0; } .ds-answer--default:hover { border-color: rgba(245,158,11,0.45); background: rgba(245,158,11,0.07); } .ds-answer--default:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f59e0b; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-answer-badge { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; flex-shrink: 0; width: 32px; height: 32px; border-radius: 2px; background: rgba(245,158,11,0.15); color: #f59e0b; font-family: 'Bebas Neue', sans-serif; font-size: 1.1rem; } .ds-answer-content { display: flex; flex-direction: column; flex: 1; } .ds-answer-name { font-size: 1.05rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.02em; } .ds-answer-country { font-family: 'Share Tech Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.7rem; opacity: 0.65; letter-spacing: 0.06em; margin-top: 1px; }

components.name: Answer Option — Correct
components.description: Quiz answer button state when this option is the correct answer (revealed after selection).
components.css: .ds-answer--correct { background: rgba(34,197,94,0.1); border-color: #22c55e; color: #86efac; cursor: default; } .ds-answer-badge--correct { background: rgba(34,197,94,0.2); color: #4ade80; } .ds-answer-icon { font-size: 1.1rem; font-weight: 700; color: #4ade80; }

components.name: Answer Option — Wrong
components.description: Quiz answer button state when the player selected this option but it is incorrect.
components.css: .ds-answer--wrong { background: rgba(239,68,68,0.1); border-color: #ef4444; color: #fca5a5; cursor: default; } .ds-answer-badge--wrong { background: rgba(239,68,68,0.2); color: #f87171; } .ds-answer-icon--wrong { color: #f87171; font-size: 1.1rem; font-weight: 700; }

components.name: TacCard
components.description: Bracketed container for structured mission data. Corner brackets replace a shadow or thick border. Used for Mission Intel (home), score card (debrief), future stats panels.
components.html:
MISSION INTEL
10 VEHICLES READY
components.css: .ds-tac-card { position: relative; background: rgba(26,39,68,0.45); border: 1px solid rgba(245,158,11,0.18); border-radius: 2px; padding: 24px 28px; } .ds-tac-corner { position: absolute; width: 14px; height: 14px; border-color: #f59e0b; } .ds-tac-corner--tl { top: -1px; left: -1px; border-top: 2px solid; border-left: 2px solid; } .ds-tac-corner--tr { top: -1px; right: -1px; border-top: 2px solid; border-right: 2px solid; } .ds-tac-corner--bl { bottom: -1px; left: -1px; border-bottom: 2px solid; border-left: 2px solid; } .ds-tac-corner--br { bottom: -1px; right: -1px; border-bottom: 2px solid; border-right: 2px solid; } .ds-tac-label { font-family: 'Share Tech Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.7rem; letter-spacing: 0.14em; color: rgba(245,158,11,0.4); margin-bottom: 16px; } .ds-tac-stat { font-family: 'Bebas Neue', sans-serif; font-size: 3.5rem; line-height: 1; color: #e2e8f0; } .ds-tac-stat-sub { font-family: 'Rajdhani', sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.1em; color: #64748b; display: block; margin-top: 4px; }

components.name: Result Panel — Correct
components.description: Feedback panel shown below answer options after a correct selection. Announces TARGET ACQUIRED, surfaces the fun fact.
components.html:
✓ TARGET ACQUIRED

◈ The M1 Abrams is powered by a gas turbine engine — the same type used in helicopters.
components.css: @keyframes ds-fadeSlideUp { from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(10px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); } } .ds-result { padding: 16px; border-radius: 2px; border: 1px solid; animation: ds-fadeSlideUp 0.25s cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1) forwards; } .ds-result--correct { background: rgba(34,197,94,0.06); border-color: rgba(34,197,94,0.3); } .ds-result-verdict { font-family: 'Bebas Neue', sans-serif; font-size: 1.15rem; letter-spacing: 0.1em; color: #4ade80; margin: 0 0 6px 0; } .ds-result-fact { font-family: 'Rajdhani', sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.5; color: #94a3b8; margin: 0; } .ds-result-glyph { color: #f59e0b; }

components.name: Progress Bar
components.description: Tracks quiz round progress. Amber fill on near-invisible amber track. Animates width on question advance.
components.css: .ds-progress-track { width: 100%; height: 3px; background: rgba(245,158,11,0.1); border-radius: 9999px; overflow: hidden; } .ds-progress-fill { height: 100%; background: #f59e0b; border-radius: 9999px; transition: width 0.4s cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); }

narrative.overview:
MatKenGame is built for a student who takes the subject seriously. The interface doesn't cheerfully reward participation — it sets conditions for focus. Every screen should feel like arriving at a place where the work happens: austere, purposeful, configured for the task at hand. The tactical aesthetic is not decoration; it is the logical visual register for a military vehicle identification exercise.

The system works in near-darkness. A deep, navy-tinted black fills the background; surfaces layer progressively lighter without ever getting bright. The sole bright element is amber — reserved strictly as an intel highlight, marking the one thing on screen that demands attention right now. Everything else recedes deliberately.

This system explicitly rejects the cheerful quiz-app idiom: no pastel bubbles, no celebratory confetti bursts, no encouragement copy written for children. The register is closer to a well-designed field manual or a serious simulator: authoritative, functional, and entirely in service of the learning that happens inside it.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black background with subtle amber grid — sets the atmospheric stage before content appears
Single amber accent used as strict intel highlight, never decoration
Three-font system: commanding display, working body, data label — each with one fixed job
Tonal depth through layered dark surfaces; zero box-shadows anywhere in the system
Corner brackets frame data containers — a structural signal, not visual flair
Feedback states (correct / wrong) are informational, not celebratory or punitive

rules.name: The Intel Highlight Rule
rules.body: Amber (#f59e0b) is reserved exclusively for active significance: what demands attention right now. It is never used as a color accent on static, decorative, or secondary elements. If amber appears on a screen and nothing is actively happening, the design is wrong.

rules.name: The No-Pure-White Rule
rules.body: Neither #ffffff nor #000000 appears in this system. All text is #e2e8f0 or dimmer; the darkest background is #070b14. Tinting neutrals toward the navy hue keeps the dark-mode palette cohesive.

rules.name: The Three Voices Rule
rules.body: Bebas Neue is the commanding voice: screen titles and primary actions only. Rajdhani is the working voice: all body, UI labels, and button text that isn't a primary CTA. Share Tech Mono is the data voice: numbers, counters, and machine-readable metadata. These roles are fixed and never swap.

rules.name: The No-Shadow Doctrine
rules.body: box-shadow is prohibited. Not ambient, not structural, not decorative. Depth comes from tonal layering: bg-abyss → header-dark → navy-command. Any need for separation is a layout or tonal problem.

dos: Do reserve Amber Intel (#f59e0b) for active significance only. Its rarity is its power.
Do use Bebas Neue exclusively for primary screen titles and CTA button labels.
Do use Share Tech Mono for all scores, counters, question indexes, and status glyphs.
Do maintain the tonal stack: Abyss → Header Dark → Command Navy. New surfaces land on one of these three steps.
Do apply the scanline overlay on all game screens to unify the atmospheric layer.
Do keep corner brackets on TacCards structural — they mark bounded data containers only.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion by wrapping animation declarations in the appropriate media query.
Do verify WCAG 2.1 AA contrast on every new text/background pairing.
donts: Don't use pastel colors, rounded bubble shapes, bouncy animations, or cheerful encouragement copy. No Kahoot, no Quizlet.
Don't use box-shadow anywhere. The No-Shadow Doctrine is absolute.
Don't add a fourth font. Three voices is the complete system.
Don't add gradient text (background-clip: text). Text is a single solid color.
Don't use border-left as a colored accent stripe on cards or list items.
Don't use glassmorphism (backdrop-filter: blur) as a visual effect.
Don't use confetti, star bursts, badge pops, coin animations, or any mobile F2P reward mechanic.
Don't let the result panel animate on re-render — the fadeSlideUp fires once on mount only.
